revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka)]][[ja:聖神社 (和泉市)]] {{Expand Japanese|聖神社 (和泉市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hijiri Shrine (Q11611103) | native_name = 聖神社 | image = Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) haiden+honden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hijiri Shrine (Q11611103) | coordinates = {{coord|34.501189|135.446954}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Hijiri no Ōkami|lt=Hijiri no Ōkami|qid=Q135524506|1=Hijiri no Ōkami}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hijiri Shrine'''|聖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|San-no-Miya|lt=San-no-Miya|qid=Q134917303|1=San-no-Miya}} ** P1001: {{ill|Q1047117|ltq=Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135009205|ltq=Q135009205|qid=Q135009205|1=Shrines receiving Hoe offering}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|ja|和泉国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11417778|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 44 ** P155: {{ill|Q11613492|ltq=Q11613492|qid=Q11613492|1=Furufu Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Wokamino Shrine|lt=Wokamino Shrine|qid=Q135039188|1=Wokamino Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q696412|ltq=Q696412|qid=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}} * {{ill|Q1047117|ltq=Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}} * {{ill|Izumi district|lt=Izumi district|ja|和泉郡|zh|和泉郡|qid=Q10982247|1=Izumi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.501189|135.446954}} == image (P18) == * Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) haiden+honden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Hijiri no Ōkami|lt=Hijiri no Ōkami|qid=Q135524506|1=Hijiri no Ōkami}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20041124114655/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=40401 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181011 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Fu-sha|lt=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11071127|ltq=Q11071127|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == heritage designation (P1435) == * {{ill|Q1188622|ltq=Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} == country (P17) == * Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 252388558 == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 10306946 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7120105006725 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00638490 == Commons category (P373) == * Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府和泉市王子町919', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) sando.jpg {{commons category|Category:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka)}} [[JA:聖神社 (和泉市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11611103}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Hijiri Shrine |画像 = [[File:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) haiden+honden.jpg|270px]]
Main sanctuary (rear right) and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumi, Osaka|Q696412|qid=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}} Ōjichō 919 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|04.4|N|135|26|49.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Hijiri Shrine (Izumi City)}} |祭神 = Hijiri Ōkami |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917326|lt=Fusha|1=Fu-sha}} |創建 = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=675|Q30523|qid=Q30523|1=675}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|qid=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} |別名 = Shinoda Myōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 10|Q2557|qid=Q2557|1=May 10}} |神事 = Chōsai, Sumō Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Hijiri Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hijiri Shrine''' (ひじりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ōjichō, {{ill|lt=Izumi, Osaka|Q696412|qid=Q696412|1=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917326|lt=Fusha|1=Fu-sha}}. It is the {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}. It is also called “Shinoda Myōjin,” “Shinoda-sha,” and “Shinoda Hijiri-sha.” === Enshrined deity === * Hijiri Ōkami (ひじりのおおかみ) Hijiri Ōkami is a {{ill|lt=child deity|Q352176|qid=Q352176|1=Mikogami}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtoshi no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, who appears as “Hijiri no Kami” in the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}.' According to the 'Izumi no Kuni Ōtori Gosha Daimyōjin narabi ni Fuchū Sōsha Hachimangū Engi,' its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji-butsu|1=UNKNOWN}} is {{ill|lt=Jizō Bosatsu|Q604687|qid=Q604687|1=Kṣitigarbha}}. {{要出典範囲|According to shrine-family tradition, the following five deities are said to be enshrined. They are apparently now accompanying deities.|date=March 2010}}. * {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|qid=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}} * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Iwanaga-hime|Q8191705|qid=Q8191705|lt=Iwanaga-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Iwanaga-hime}} ===History=== [[File:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) honden2.jpg|180px|thumb|Main sanctuary]] At this shrine, there are two traditions: {{要出典範囲|one says that during {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}’s eastern expedition, {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} was enshrined here,|date=May 2023}} and the other says that on August 15 of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=675|Q30523|qid=Q30523|1=675}}), by imperial prayer order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|qid=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, Shinoda no Obito, an immigrant {{ill|lt=clan|Q211503|qid=Q211503|1=Clan}} of {{ill|lt=toraijin|Q11561926|qid=Q11561926|lt=continental origin|1=Toraijin}}, enshrined Hijiri Ōkami. On May 7, {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kansha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and on August 13 of the same year received {{ill|lt=deity rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} advancement from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade ('{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'). In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' enforced in {{ill|lt=Enchō|UNKNOWN|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed in the '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}). In the medieval '{{ill|lt=Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|qid=Q11420490|lt=Izumi no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}},' its deity rank is “Senior First Rank.” In the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the Shinoda clan served successively as chief priests, but because they sided with the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|qid=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}} in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|qid=Q264012|lt=Northern and Southern Courts period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, they later lost that position, and in {{ill|lt=Enbun|Q1145071|qid=Q1145071|1=Enbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1360|Q6150|qid=Q6150|1=1360}}), Wada Kurando became chief priest. From the Nanboku-chō period, together with {{ill|lt=Ōtori Taisha|Q705151|qid=Q705151|lt=Ōtori Shrine|1=Ōtori taisha}}, {{ill|lt=Izumi Anashi Shrine|Q11554908|qid=Q11554908|1=Izumianashi Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Tsumekawa Shrine|Q11597137|qid=Q11597137|1=Tsugawa Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Hine Shrine|Q11509543|qid=Q11509543|1=Hine Shrine}} in the same {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|qid=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}, it came to be called one of the “Five Great Deity Shrines.” It held the third rank among shrines of Izumi Province, though the designation “Sannomiya” does not appear in medieval records. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|qid=Q6688|1=1575}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} confirmed holdings of 1,100 {{ill|lt=koku|Q855048|qid=Q855048|1=Koku}}, but in year 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|qid=Q6736|1=1585}}), during {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}’s {{ill|lt=Kishū campaign#Battle in Izumi|Q10855721|qid=Q10855721|lt=attack on Negoro|1=Battle of Kishuu}}, it suffered war fire and its lands were confiscated. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|qid=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} had {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|qid=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} supervise construction of shrines and temples in various places, and this shrine was also rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|qid=Q6738|1=1604}}). Existing buildings from that time are the main sanctuary of the main shrine and the sanctuaries of the subsidiary Sansha, Taki Shrine, and Hiraoka Shrine; they are designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} and tangible cultural properties of {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. There was also Manshō-in of the {{ill|lt=Shingon sect|Q550182|qid=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} as its {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}, with a {{ill|lt=five-storied pagoda|Q11372713|qid=Q11372713|1=Five-storied pagoda}} and bell tower. In {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1796|Q7818|qid=Q7818|1=1796}}), a dispute occurred between the {{ill|lt=shrine family|Q11588367|qid=Q11588367|1=Shake (social class)}} and shrine monks. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, due to the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Manshō-in was abolished. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked as a village shrine, and in March {{ill|lt=1943|Q18623|qid=Q18623|1=1943}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 18), it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917326|lt=Fusha|1=Fu-sha}}. The “Naka-Izumi Kaidō” extending from Sukematsu-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumiōtsu|Q873553|qid=Q873553|1=Izumiōtsu}}, is also called the “Nubiki Road,” derived from the tradition that Shinoda Daimyōjin, after landing at Sukematsu-hama, pulled cloth while heading to Hijiri Shrine. A monument for the “Naka-Izumi Kaidō” stands at the intersection with the {{ill|lt=Kishū Kaidō|Q11605050|qid=Q11605050|1=Kishū Kaidō}}. === Precincts === [[画像:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) honden1.jpg|180px|thumb|Main sanctuary (Important Cultural Property)]] [[画像:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) Taki-jinja+San-jinja.jpg|180px|thumb|Subsidiary Sansha Shrine (right) and Taki Shrine (left), both Important Cultural Properties]] [[画像:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) Hiraoka-jinja honden.jpg|180px|thumb|Main sanctuary of subsidiary Hiraoka Shrine (prefectural designated tangible cultural property)]] * Main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|qid=Q6738|1=1604}}) with {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|qid=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} and {{ill|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|Q1882775|qid=Q1882775|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} as magistrate. A shrine building of {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with {{ill|lt=cypress-bark roofing|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}, 3 bays front and 3 bays depth, decorated on the front with a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=chidori-hafu|1=UNKNOWN}}, and with a 1-bay projecting porch ({{ill|lt=kōhai|Q11415970|qid=Q11415970|1=kōhai}}) with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|qid=Q977293|1=Karahafu}} eaves. Designated a specially protected structure on April 15, {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|qid=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13) under the {{ill|lt=Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|Q11411552|qid=Q11411552|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law}}, and became an Important Cultural Property under enforcement of the {{ill|lt=Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Q1188502|qid=Q1188502|1=Cultural Properties Protection Law}} in {{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|qid=Q18597|1=1950}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 25). * Haiden * Shrine office * Hijiri {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|qid=Q337624|1=Acala}} - a remnant of {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. * Worship-from-afar site - for {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|qid=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. Built in modern times. === Affiliated and subsidiary shrines === * Sansha Shrine (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in Keichō 9 (1604) with Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto as magistrate. Its polychrome decoration and ornate styling show features of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Momoyama period|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine building is a three-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} with cypress-bark roof. A three-bay Kasuga-zukuri (three front pillar intervals) is rare nationwide. Relocated to current site in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6). * Taki Shrine (Important Cultural Property) - Rebuilt in Keichō 9 (1604) with Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto as magistrate. Building is one-bay Kasuga-zukuri with cypress-bark roof. Kōhai is made with karahafu eaves. Relocated to current site in 1873 (Meiji 6). * Hiraoka Shrine (Osaka Prefecture-designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural property|Q2901860|qid=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Rebuilt in Keichō 9 (1604) with Toyotomi Hideyori and Katagiri Katsumoto as magistrate. Main sanctuary is one-bay Kasuga-zukuri with cypress-bark roof. * Kotohira Shrine - Deity: {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}-no-Kami * Itsukushima Shrine - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishima-hime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * Inari mounds - There are many Inari mounds enshrining {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|qid=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Main sanctuary, with: 1 inner shrine structure * Main sanctuary of subsidiary Sansha Shrine * Main sanctuary of subsidiary Taki Shrine === Osaka Prefecture-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * Main sanctuary of subsidiary Hiraoka Shrine === Important Art Object === * Portable shrine (mikoshi) - bears an ink inscription dated February 28, {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 15 ({{ill|lt=1483|Q6637|qid=Q6637|1=1483}}).[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai20190403.pdf Designated Cultural Properties of Izumi City] === Rituals === * Annual festival: May 10 === Access === * Get off at {{ill|lt=Kita-Shinoda Station|Q856975|qid=Q856975|1=Kita-Shinoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Hanwa Line|Q1154800|qid=Q1154800|1=Hanwa Line}} === Surroundings === * {{ill|lt=Kumano Kaidō|Q11568976|qid=Q11568976|1=Kumano Kaidō}} - The shrine’s {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} and {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|qid=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} are along the Kumano Kaidō. * {{ill|lt=Shinoda Ōji|Q11603815|qid=Q11603815|1=Shinoda Ōji Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Shinodamori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|Q11386029|qid=Q11386029|1=Shinodanomori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Furufu Shrine|Q11613492|qid=Q11613492|1=Furufu Shrine}} (ふるふじんじゃ) - enshrines a fossilized white fox (“bake-ishi”). * Shinoda no Mori Furusato-kan / Shinoda no Mori Kagamiike Historic Park - Walking from the shrine grounds toward Kagamiike, there is “Nezumi-zaka,” said to be where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Abe no Yasuna|1=UNKNOWN}} (father of {{ill|lt=Abe no Seimei|Q1150151|qid=Q1150151|1=Abe no Seimei}}) met his mother {{ill|lt=Kuzunoha|Q1651785|qid=Q1651785|1=Kuzunoha}}. * {{ill|lt=Inryō-ji (Izumi City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Inryō-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|Q11417846|qid=Q11417846|1=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun}} * {{ill|lt=Ikegami-Sone Site|Q11551440|qid=Q11551440|1=Ikegami-Sone Site}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Hijiri Shrine history === External links === *[https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/hijirijinja.html Hijiri Shrine – Cultural Properties of Izumi City] *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hijiri Shrine (Q11611103)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hijiri Shrine (Q11611103)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumi, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Izumi, Osaka]]
